title: Deeper Wisdom
scripture_text: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.
reflection: A closed door is not always a punishment. Sometimes it is protection. Sometimes it is redirection. (The fourth hearing of this day.)
body:
They said no. The offer did not come. The person you loved chose someone else. The thing you worked for went to someone else. The door you had been pushing against for years quietly, finally, definitely closed.

This is one of the hardest kinds of loss, because it is not a death. It is a non-birth. A future that was alive in your mind, and is now not.

Grieve it. Do not skip this. The grief is real, even though nothing happened that you can point to.

Then, slowly, look around. The Lord is not behind you, pointing at the closed door. He is ahead of you, opening another. You cannot see it yet. Trust that it is there.

Wisdom is not information. Wisdom is the slow, lived-in knowing. It is the difference between a book about swimming and a person who has swum. The book has the facts. The person has the body-knowledge, the water-knowledge, the muscle-and-bone memory.

You have lived enough to have wisdom. You have made enough decisions to know what the wrong one felt like. You have loved enough to know what it cost. You have lost enough to know what you would do differently next time. The wisdom is in the living. The wisdom is the long sediment of the years.

The Lord gives more. Ask Him. He gives generously, without scolding. He is the one who gives wisdom like He gives breath.

The wisdom is in the next small choice. Make the small choice. The Lord will meet you there.

Wisdom is not information. Wisdom is the slow, lived-in knowing. It is the difference between a book about swimming and a person who has swum. The book has the facts. The person has the body-knowledge, the water-knowledge, the muscle-and-bone memory.

You have lived enough to have wisdom. You have made enough decisions to know what the wrong one felt like. You have loved enough to know what it cost. You have lost enough to know what you would do differently next time. The wisdom is in the living. The wisdom is the long sediment of the years.

The Lord gives more. Ask Him. He gives generously, without scolding. He is the one who gives wisdom like He gives breath.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

The wisdom is in the next small choice. Make the small choice. The Lord will meet you there.

This is the fourth time. You have gone deeper now than you went the first time. The well is deeper. The water is still. The Lord is at the bottom. Keep going.
prayer: Lord, this door has closed. I do not understand. Walk with me toward the next one. Amen.
